diary:
Woke up to actual sunshine—rare enough in Seattle that I savored my home brew a little longer. The morning shift flew by; we were slammed because half the city decided to camp at bars for the USA-Wales match. I snuck away for lunch and caught the second half at this packed dive downtown, everyone losing it. Back at the shop, I spent the afternoon training Maya on milk steaming; she's getting there, but her pour technique still needs work.

After my shift, I hit Elliott Bay Books instead of heading straight home. Grabbed three novels I've been meaning to read, got lost in the stacks for longer than planned. The evening really came together though—paddled out on Lake Union around dusk with the light hitting the water just right. My shoulders needed that. Board games at Tam's place in Fremont wrapped things up; we played Catan until nearly midnight. Fell asleep feeling that good kind of tired.
thought_process: I was actually in a better mood than I expected for a Monday. The weather helped—that sunshine just changes everything. The World Cup energy downtown was infectious, even if it meant chaos at work. I decided to skip the direct bus home after my shift because I knew I'd been cooped up; the Light Rail closure meant I had to think differently about routing anyway, so I figured why not detour to the bookstore? It felt indulgent but necessary. The kayaking was the real anchor though—I'd been mentally tense about training Maya, worried I wasn't explaining things clearly enough, so getting out on the water cleared my head. By the time I got to board game night, I was genuinely present instead of just going through motions.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
